name: Salmon Parisienne
recipeIngredient: 213 g Canned Alaska salmon (pink or red)
2 Fresh tomatoes, skinned and de-seeded, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the canned salmon, reserving the juice. Break the fish into flakes and set aside.
2. Place the salmon juice into a dish along with the scallops and wine. Cover with cling film and puncture twice. Cook on MEDIUM POWER for 3 minutes.
3. Chop the scallops and mix them into the flaked salmon.
4. Melt the butter in a dish on HIGH POWER for 30 seconds. Stir in the flour, cook for a further 30 seconds on HIGH POWER.
5. Add the liquor from the scallops to the flour, mixing well to prevent lumps forming.
6. Cook on HIGH POWER for 1 minute. Stir the sauce into the salmon and scallop mixture along with the parsley, tomatoes, and fromage frais or cream. Season to taste.
7. Pile equal amounts of the mixture into shell-shaped dishes.
8. Mix together the breadcrumbs and cheese. Sprinkle these over the scallops.
9. Arrange the dishes on a microwave-safe tray. Cook on MEDIUM POWER for 10 minutes.
10. Brown under a conventional grill, if desired. Serve with salad.
